summary: Audit and remove 'any' from shared and core code paths
problem_statement: Widespread use of 'any' across the codebase defeats TypeScript's type system, hiding bugs that only surface at runtime. Shared utilities, hooks, and core business logic are the highest-leverage targets because every consumer inherits the type weakness. Removing 'any' from these paths restores compiler-enforced safety where it matters most.
in_scope: Audit all shared/core TypeScript files for uses of 'any' (explicit and implicit)
Replace 'any' with explicit types, generics, or 'unknown' with narrowing in shared utilities, hooks, and core business logic
Define or import missing type definitions where 'any' was used as a placeholder
Ensure npm run check passes with no new type errors after replacements
out_of_scope: Removing 'any' from third-party type declarations or generated code
Removing 'any' from code paths not touched by R2 or outside shared/core modules
Changing runtime behaviour — all replacements must be type-level only
Component prop typing — handled separately in P2-R2-06
requirements: No explicit 'any' remains in shared/core code paths after this task
All replacements preserve existing runtime behaviour
npm run check passes with no new type errors introduced
acceptance_criteria: npm run check passes with no new type errors introduced
Shared/core code paths contain no explicit 'any' usage
No runtime behaviour changes result from the type replacements
dependencies: P1-R2-01 — ESLint with strict TypeScript rules must be active to catch implicit any
risks: Some 'any' usages may mask complex type relationships that require non-trivial generic signatures to replace correctly
implementation_notes: Start with a grep/search for explicit 'any' in shared/ and core paths. Use the @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any rule from P1-R2-01 to surface remaining violations. Prefer 'unknown' with type guards over broad union types where the actual type is genuinely dynamic.
